exampleSentences: Chị đã sắp đặt mọi thứ cho bữa tiệc sinh nhật tối nay.
Anh sẽ sắp đặt lại lịch trình để gặp em vào chiều mai.
Em nhờ mẹ sắp đặt bàn ăn trước khi khách đến.
notes:
Sắp đặt = to arrange or set something up, often with intention behind it. It can also mean to deliberately plan or orchestrate a situation.

Used for events, schedules, meetings, or even situations and relationships. It often sounds more intentional or calculated than sắp xếp (which is more neutral, just organizing).

It can sometimes imply “arrange behind the scenes” or even “set up” in a slightly suspicious way, depending on context.

translation.en: We need to arrange for the party.

content.sentence: Chúng ta cần ___ cho bữa tiệc.

definitions.en: To arrange or organize something in a particular order.
definitions.es: Organizar o disponer algo en un orden particular.
definitions.vi: Sắp xếp hoặc tổ chức một cái gì đó theo một thứ tự nhất định.

example.en: I am rearranging the furniture in the room.
example.es: Estoy reorganizando los muebles en la habitación.
example.vi: Tôi đang sắp đặt lại đồ đạc trong phòng.

exampleSentencesTranslated: She has arranged everything for the birthday party tonight.
He will arrange the schedule again to meet you tomorrow afternoon.
I asked my mom to arrange the dining table before the guests arrive.

translation.en: We need to arrange the desk.

content.sentence: Chúng ta cần ___ lại bàn làm việc.

notes_md:
**Sắp đặt** = to arrange or set something up, often with intention behind it. It can also mean to deliberately plan or orchestrate a situation.

Used for events, schedules, meetings, or even situations and relationships. It often sounds more intentional or calculated than **sắp xếp** (which is more neutral, just organizing).


It can sometimes imply “arrange behind the scenes” or even “set up” in a slightly suspicious way, depending on context.
